notes: pi has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool. This cell exercises the agent-agnostic trailing-? text-classifier path (state_classifier.go decision 2a): when an assistant turn ends with a question whose terminal text ends in '?', the classifier extracts it into LastAssistantText → IsWaitingForUserInput → the session transitions working→WAITING at turn end, NOT working→ready. This is the ONLY pi cell that legitimately enters the waiting state — the load-bearing assertion is the turn_end phase asserting expected_state:waiting. RECORDED IN SCRIPT MODE (interactive tmux REPL), NOT headless `pi --print`: in headless print-mode pi answers and the process exits immediately at turn end, so the waiting episode is lost to a process_exited race. In the live REPL the session SITS in waiting after the question-turn completes (no user reply is sent) until the driver tears down the tmux session — so the waiting state is observable and dwells. The driver sends ONE prompt that reliably elicits a single trailing-? clarifying question, wait_turn (the turn ends stopReason=stop), then a trailing sleep to let the session dwell in waiting, then teardown. NO reply is sent to the question. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY (same workaround as multi-turn-conversation / user-esc-interrupt): the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARC must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); pid_bind, turn_end, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant question wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: one ready→working→WAITING arc on one rollout session_id that does NOT settle back to ready before teardown, a single process_exited, and a final waiting state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Prompt asking for one clarifying question → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
PID bound to the rollout session
The assistant reply ends in a question mark, so the turn settles working→WAITING (the trailing-? path) rather than working→ready — the agent is waiting for the user to answer
No reply is sent; the session dwells in waiting until the REPL is torn down → a single process_exited on the rollout session, and the state never settles back to ready before teardown
